event: biopsy specimens confirmed the diagnosis of disseminated peritoneal adenomucinosis (DPAM)
peritoneal lesions originated from perforated mucinous cystadenoma of the appendix
advised to undergo peritonectomy and bilateral oophorectomy
referred for consultation on fertility preservation options
decided to undergo IVF and embryo cryopreservation procedures
embryos cryopreserved at the 2 pronuclear stage
Institutional Ethical Committee approved the embryo cryopreservation procedure
postoperatively, the patient received heated intraperitoneal infusions of mitomycin C
intra abdominal fluid accumulation with multiple peritoneal lesions
administration of 2 mg tid E2 valerate (Progynova; Schering Health Care Ltd., West Sussex, UK)
vaginal administration of micronized progesterone (200 mg tid, Utrogestan; Besins International Laboratories, Paris, France)
3 of the 9 surviving thawed embryos were transferred
difficult to anatomically identify the uterine cervix on ultrasonography (USG)
urinary bladder appeared to be "smeared" all over the uterus, up to the fundus
gradual elevation of creatinine (up to 1.4 mL/DL)
single course of Betametasone (2 doses of 12 mg, IM-24 hours apart)
abdominal USG demonstrated mild bilateral hydronephrosis
urinary bladder covered the anterior part of the uterus
Doppler USG ruled out renal vein thrombosis
Cystoscopy revealed a much distorted bladder trigone
pigtail (6 F/26 cm) was inserted into the left ureter
blood creatinine had sharply risen to 3.1 mL/DL
urology consultant suggested nephrostomy by angiography
patient reported immediate relief of the flank pain
could urinate at will immediately after delivery
received 2 infusions of packed cells due to postpartum hemorrhage
rest of the postpartum course was uneventful
blood creatinine level returned to normal
referred to an oncologist, hematologist and urologist for further follow-up
